panels.description: Medium shot: The narrator pacing in a dimly lit, dusty Victorian parlor. He looks distraught and anxious, clutching his hair. Shadows stretch across the walls. Atmospheric lighting from a cracked window.

bubbles.text: How can I be away from her? My cousin is brave, but he lacks the circumspection to survive this.

panels.description: Medium close-up: The narrator turns away from the curate in a cluttered children's schoolroom. The curate looks pathetic and desperate in the background, out of focus. The narrator's face is hardened with irritation.

bubbles.text: I cannot stand his selfish despair any longer. I must be alone.

panels.description: Wide shot: Through a dusty attic window, the thick, unnatural Black Smoke coils like a living creature outside, choking the landscape and blotting out the sun.

bubbles.text: Hemmed in. The Black Smoke drifts ever closer, a suffocating wall of death.

panels.description: Dynamic wide action shot: A Martian tripod machine towers over the fields in the distance, venting a high-pressure jet of superheated steam. Walls of the house are shattering; glass shards fly into the room as the curate recoils in terror, clutching a scalded hand.
Wide shot: The landscape looking north, now covered in a layer of strange black dust, but patches of eerie, glowing crimson vegetation have begun to sprout, breaking through the devastation.

bubbles.text: The smoke is gone. The red weed takes its place. We can leave.

panels.description: Close-up: The narrator's face, eyes wide with a mix of haunting regret and newfound determination. The lighting is soft and reflective, highlighting the lines of grief on his face.

bubbles.text: I should have acted sooner. I should have saved her. If only I could go back.
